name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 28.07.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 28.07.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Sowing Status: As on 25 July, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 829.64 lakh ha (including 55.16 lakh ha (105% of normal area) in Sugarcane) as compared to 797.91 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 75.6 percent of the normal area has been sown  under  the  Kharif  season  crops.  The  status  of  sowing  under  Rice,  Pulses  and  Coarse Cereals is better than in the same period of last year.
texts.text: Kharif Sowing Status: As on 25 July, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 829.64 lakh ha (including 55.16 lakh ha (105% of normal area) in Sugarcane) as compared to 797.91 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 75.6 percent of the normal area has been sown  under  the  Kharif  season  crops.  The  status  of  sowing  under  Rice,  Pulses  and  Coarse Cereals is better than in the same period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum  temperatures  are  likely  to  be  above  normal  by  2-3°C  over northwest India (except Jammu & Kashmir), Bihar and northeast India. These are likely to be below normal or near normal over rest parts of the country.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum  temperatures  are  likely  to  be  above  normal  by  2-3°C  over northwest India (except Jammu & Kashmir), Bihar and northeast India. These are likely to be below normal or near normal over rest parts of the country.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution: From June 01- July 28, 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 7% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:  (-)23%; (ii) Northwest India: 19%; (iii) Central India: 25%; and South Peninsula: 2%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution: From June 01- July 28, 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 7% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:  (-)23%; (ii) Northwest India: 19%; (iii) Central India: 25%; and South Peninsula: 2%.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A fresh spell of Heavy to very heavy rainfall likely to commence over Western Himalayan region during 29th-31st July.
texts.text: A fresh spell of Heavy to very heavy rainfall likely to commence over Western Himalayan region during 29th-31st July.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Heavy to very Heavy rainfall likely over Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan during next 4 days with isolated extremely heavy rainfall over East Rajasthan on 28th & 29th, Gujarat Region and West Madhya Pradesh on 28th July.
texts.text: Heavy to very Heavy rainfall likely over Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan during next 4 days with isolated extremely heavy rainfall over East Rajasthan on 28th & 29th, Gujarat Region and West Madhya Pradesh on 28th July.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  likely  to  continue  over  Arunachal  Pradesh,  Assam  and Meghalaya during next 7 days.
texts.text: Heavy  to  very  heavy  rainfall  likely  to  continue  over  Arunachal  Pradesh,  Assam  and Meghalaya during next 7 days.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Fairly widespread to widespread rainfall likely over northwest, northeast, eastern India. Scattered to fairly widespread rainfall likely over central and peninsular India.
texts.text: Fairly widespread to widespread rainfall likely over northwest, northeast, eastern India. Scattered to fairly widespread rainfall likely over central and peninsular India.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.
texts.text: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Inputs Situation
texts.text: Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3. Water Reservoir Status :  As on 24 th  July 2025, the total live water storage in 161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 110.86 Billion Cubic Meter, which is 61% of total live storage capacity (182.46 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 159 percent of last year's storage and 153 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage  position  is  better than  the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water Reservoir Status :  As on 24 th  July 2025, the total live water storage in 161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 110.86 Billion Cubic Meter, which is 61% of total live storage capacity (182.46 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 159 percent of last year's storage and 153 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during  the  corresponding  period.  The  overall  storage  position  is  better than  the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the
texts.text: Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: text
texts.orig: range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.
texts.text: range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.
texts.text: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5. Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6. Pests & Diseases: Intensity of pests and diseases, at some fields, has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Arecanut (Inflorescence Caterpillar) in Tamil Nadu and Rice (Southern Rice Black Streaked Dwarf Virus (SRBSDV)) in Haryana. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases: Intensity of pests and diseases, at some fields, has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Arecanut (Inflorescence Caterpillar) in Tamil Nadu and Rice (Southern Rice Black Streaked Dwarf Virus (SRBSDV)) in Haryana. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (17 July - 23 July2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 14%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June  to  23 rd July  2025) is  higher  than LPA by 5%.
texts.text:  During the week (17 July - 23 July2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 14%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June  to  23 rd July  2025) is  higher  than LPA by 5%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Overall,  near  normal to  above  normal  rainfall  activity  is  likely  over  most  parts  of  east  & northeast  and  near  normal  over  many  parts  of  northwest  India;  below  normal  over remaining parts of the country during the week.
texts.text:  Overall,  near  normal to  above  normal  rainfall  activity  is  likely  over  most  parts  of  east  & northeast  and  near  normal  over  many  parts  of  northwest  India;  below  normal  over remaining parts of the country during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The monsoon trough at mean sea level passes through Sri Ganganagar, Churu, center of low-pressure  area  over  northwest  Madhya  Pradesh  &  adjoining  east  Rajasthan,  Satna, Deoria, Ranchi, Haldia and thence east-southeast-wards to the northeast Bay of Bengal.
texts.text:  The monsoon trough at mean sea level passes through Sri Ganganagar, Churu, center of low-pressure  area  over  northwest  Madhya  Pradesh  &  adjoining  east  Rajasthan,  Satna, Deoria, Ranchi, Haldia and thence east-southeast-wards to the northeast Bay of Bengal.

texts.orig:  Soil moisture as on 25 July 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in major  parts  of  Rajasthan,  M.P,  Odisha,  Chhattisgarh,  Jharkhand,  Southern  West  Bengal, Haryana, Punjab, Kerala, Karnataka and Western & Bundelkhand region of U.P.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  It was lower than average of past 9 years in Northern and Central parts of Bihar, Eastern parts of U.P., Northern parts of West Bengal, Assam and in parts of Saurashtra region of Gujarat.
